hypothesis_en: Mr President, my first word is to contradict the previous speaker, who has had a great political, not technical, presidency, and it has been under difficult conditions, precisely under the conditions in which the March 11 massacre in Madrid took place, and I believe that the first measures you have taken, the arming of the counter-terrorist coordinator, are going in the right direction to fight against that scourge, which is terrorism, which affects our public freedoms. Mr President, you have reached an agreement on the European Constitution. This Parliament is going to vote on that text in the coming weeks, and therefore everyone knows my position on the subject, and I do not want to advance anything on that subject, but I congratulate you because it was not easy to reach an agreement and, in the end, that agreement, that Constitution that Europe needs, is on the table. It is not easy either, we have a great candidate and, above all, I think we have to make a political effort now to put the Commission where it should be at the centre of the Community method, at the centre of the life of the European Union, and therefore I hope that this important vote in favour of the candidate proposed by the European Council will also go in the right direction to reconstruct those relations between the Commission and the European Parliament which are so important. Mr Tishok, the most important thing has been enlargement. When, a few weeks ago, we celebrated here the anniversary of Monet and Spinelli, my group, the honour of representing you in that debate, and I thought that if Monet and Spinelli were sitting here in these benches, they would think that the enlargement that has been achieved under the Irish presidency, that sewing the two Europeans together, is the most important thing we have done in the next few years. And that will always be in its own right. And let me say one thing. There is a lot of talk here about big countries and small countries. I think it is a pernicious distinction that has never existed in European history. Why is it a big country or a small country? Thank you very much.
reference_en: thank you very much President first of all let me contradict what the previous speaker has said it has been a great political presidency not a technical presidency and you've conducted this in difficult times difficult conditions the massacre of the eleventh of March in Madrid and the first measures you adopted in the counter-terrorist mode are moving in the right direction to fight its position over the next few weeks on this and I certainly don't want to anticipate anything in that regard but I would congratulate you because it wasn't easy to arrive at an agreement and at the end of the day the Constitution unifying Europe is now before us on the table I'd like to congratulate you President office for the proposal to appoint Mr Barroso as a President-designate of the Commission w= it ha= it wasn't easy to achieve we have a great candidate and I think we should make a great political effort now to put the Commission where it's supposed to be in the centre of the European community in the centre of the life of the European Union and I hope that this important vote in favour of the candidate put forward by the European Council will also go in the right direction to re-establish these relations between the institutions (1.12) (1.07) ~Teesutch Taoiseach I think the most important achievement has been enlargement a few weeks ago (1.03) here we celebrated the a= anniversary of Monnet and Spinelli and my Group I had the honour of representing my Group in that debate and now had Spinelli and Monnet been sitting here they would hav= hav= have seen the Irish presidency contributing to this great achievement and that w= is something that I think you w= w= will re= remember f= for a long time this distinction between large and small Member States has never existed you know why a country is big or small because of their size o= of of GDP of their territory or their the number of p= people well no I think a a country is great or small if it has ambition if its people have ambitions and you Taoiseach have shown you to be great Europeans a big country in Europe thank you
source_es: gracias señor Presidente y mi primera palabra es para contradecir al orador anterior la suya ha sido una gran Presidencia política no técnica y lo ha sido en condiciones difíciles precisamente en condiciones en que se ha producido la masacre del once de marzo en Madrid y creo que las primeras medidas que ustedes han tomado el nombramiento del Coordinador antiterrorista van en la buena dirección para luchar contra esa lacra que es el terrorismo que afecta a nuestras libertades públicas yo creo Señor Presidente me quiero referir a ello ustedes han conseguido un acuerdo sobre la Constitución europea este Parlamento se va a pronunciar en las próximas semanas sobre ese texto y por tanto todo el mundo sabe mi postura sobre el tema yo no quiero adelantar nada sobre ese tema pero felicit= le felicito porque no era fácil llegar a un acuerdo y al final ese acuerdo esa Constitución que necesita Europa está en estos momentos sobre la mesa sí quiero felicitarle señor Presidente por la propuesta de designar al Señor Durão Barroso como candidato a ser Presidente de la Comisión no era sencillo tampoco tenemos un gran candidato y sobre todo yo creo que tenemos que hacer ahora un esfuerzo político para situar a la Comisión donde debe estar en el centro del método comunitario en el centro de la vida de la Unión Europea y por tanto yo espero que esa votación importante en favor del candidato que nos propone el Consejo Europeo vaya también en la buena dirección de recomponer esas relaciones entre Comisión y Parlamento Europeo que tan importantes son pero yo creo que toda su Presidencia Señor Presiden= Señor Taoiseach lo más importante lo más importante ha sido la ampliación cuando hace unas pocas semanas celebramos aquí conmemoramos el aniversario de Monnet y de Spinelli mi Grupo hubo el honor de representarle en ese debate y pensé que si Monnet y ~Espineli estuvieran aquí sentados en estos bancos pensarían que la ampliación que se ha logrado bajo Presidencia irlandesa es ese coser las dos Europas en la expresión de Geremek es lo más importante que hemos hecho en los próximos años y eso estará en su haber siempre y déjeme decir una cosa aquí se habla mucho de países grandes y países pequeños yo creo que es una distinción perniciosa que nunca ha existido en la historia europea por qué es un país grande o pequeño por su población por su territorio por su PIB no un país es grande o es pequeño cuando sus dirigentes y sus pueblos sus ciudadanos tienen ambición tienen visión y usted Señor Taoiseach e Irlanda han sido en esta presidencia grandes en Europa muchas gracias
